MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-20-2016 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, PROPOUNDED BY DEFENDANT MILLWHEEL NORTH INC. IS GRANTED. ON OR BEFORE JUNE 10, 2016 AT 5. P.M., PLAINTIFF MILLWHEEL NORTH OWNERS' ASS'N IS ORDERED SERVE VERIFIED SUBSTANTIVE FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, NOS. 1 - 94, EXCEPT FOR SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 59, 80, AND 87. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS AND ASSERTIONS BASED ON THE ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE AND/OR ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE SHALL BE SUBSTANTIATED IN ITS FURTHER RESPONSES BY STATING SUFFICIENT FACTUAL INFORMATION FOR THE PROPOUNDING PARTY TO EVALUATE THE MERITS OF SUCH CLAIMS. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: REBECCA WOODSON; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

MEMO OF P & A IN REPLY TO DEFENDANTS OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 60044709) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MILLWHEEL NORTH OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT MUTUAL BENEFIT CORPORATION
ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-17-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MILLWHEEL NORTH OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, MOTION SEEKING LEAVE TO FILE AND SERVE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE TWO NEW CLAIMS ARE BASED ON THE SAME SET OF FACTS AS THE EXISTING CLAIMS AND DO NOT APPEAR TO REQUIRE ANY ADDITIONAL DISCOVERY OR FACTUAL INVESTIGATION. BECAUSE THE TWO NEW CLAIMS INVOLVE THE SAME PORTIONS OF THE PROPERTY AS THE EXISTING CLAIMS, TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF HAS STANDING TO BRING THE EXISTING CLAIMS, PLAINTIFF HAS STANDING TO BRING THE TWO NEW CLAIMS. DEFENDANT MILLWHEEL NORTH INC. HAS NOT SHOWN THAT EITHER OR BOTH OF THE TWO NEW CLAIMS ARE UNTENABLE OR THAT IT WOULD SUFFER ANY PREJUDICE BY THE GRANTING OF THIS MOTION. THE POLICY OF LIBERALLY ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS STRONGLY FAVORS GRANTING THE MOTION. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-28-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-01-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion Seeking Leave To File And Serve First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-17-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF MILLWHEEL NORTH OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, Motion Seeking Leave To File And Serve First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. The two new claims are based on the same set of facts as the existing claims and do not appear to require any additional discovery or factual investigation. Because the two new claims involve the same portions of the property as the existing claims, to the extent that plaintiff has standing to bring the existing claims, plaintiff has standing to bring the two new claims. Defendant Millwheel North Inc. has not shown that either or both of the two new claims are untenable or that it would suffer any prejudice by the granting of this motion. The policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings strongly favors granting the motion. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-28-2016 Continued To May-22-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL R. MORRIS OF THE MILLER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-437-1800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRIAN F. ZIMMERMAN OF ZIMMERMAN & KAHANOWITCH, TEL. NO. 1-818-710-7777 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Milwheel North, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of the issue of standing to assert a claim for violation of interunit noise per Civil Code section 896(g)(6) is denied in its entirety. Defendant has not presented evidence which demonstrates that the buildings, specifically the buildings' common areas, were built in compliance with then existing acoustic building standards. Assuming that defendant satisfied its intitial summary adjudication burend, plaintiff has presented sufficient evidence creating triable issues whether it has a claim for violation of section 896(g)(6) and whether the violation has caused damage to the buildings' common areas. (UMF 15, 16; Plaintiff's Facts 33, 35-41.) Defendant's position that in order to assert a claim for violation of section 896(g)(6) plaintiff must present evidence of physical damage to the buildings is unsupported by legal authority. The Declaration of Restrictions identifies bearing walls, subfloors, and unfinished floors as common areas. (UMF 33.) The declarations of Mr. Posard and Mr. Daroux create a triable issue whether the common areas were constructed in compliance with then existing acoustic building standards.(Plaintiff's Facts 34-40). Plaintiff paid to have the defects investigated and is entitled to recover the reasonable value of repairing the violation. (Civil Code section 944; Plaintiff's Fact 41). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
